page: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processors. This x86_64 architecture platform features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz, supported by 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed local instance storage directly attached to the bare-metal, hypervisor-free environment. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores falling into the strong category. The server excels in memory bandwidth, achieving top-tier PassMark memory scores and strong read and write bandwidths. Database and caching operations are highly efficient, as demonstrated by strong Redis SET operations and PassMark database performance. However, single-core benchmarks, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core, remain in the average tier. Additionally, while prompt processing for large language models is average, text generation performance is weak to poor, highlighting a tradeoff for real-time generative AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.metal offers high resource density for compute-heavy workloads that require direct access to physical hardware and high-speed local storage. The inclusion of 3600 GB of NVMe SSD storage eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput data operations. This server is highly suited for workloads such as high-performance web serving, software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors running at 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on a 25 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database execution capabilities, though single-core performance and LLM text generation remain average to weak. The absence of virtualized hypervisor overhead combined with high-speed local storage makes this server highly efficient for intensive multi-threaded applications. It is best suited for high-performance database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and compute-heavy workloads requiring direct hardware access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.metal is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and dedicated multi-core processing for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processors with 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores
3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier memory bandwidth
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database operations
